mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict downward price moves during the first month of recession.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household demand, compressing corporate revenue growth expectations and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative forward returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above 105, equity markets price in strong corporate profitability, generating positive forward returns as supply-side momentum translates into earnings beats.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with high capacity utilization
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 for three consecutive months, indicating that supply-side overheating triggers inflation fears that outweigh earnings optimism.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading exceeding 2.5% annualized triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing higher discount rates and generating negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of the time following CPI prints above 2.5%, demonstrating that inflation data is decoupled from discount rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the total addressable market for corporate revenues, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth remains positive while S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, indicating a breakdown in the growth-earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong non-farm payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market imbalances that historically precede earnings revisions, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal consistently predicts positive returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery environments, a sharp rebound in industrial production above its 12-month moving average signals rapid capacity restoration, driving immediate positive equity returns as risk appetite rebounds.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds but equity markets decline for more than 60% of the time, indicating that supply recovery is priced in or overshadowed by demand-side constraints.
